name: Diego exports the /quotes pipeline as a CSV from the page header
description: Bookkeeper's annual ask: a CSV of every quote sent this year, with status, customer name, total (cents-correct dollars in the export), sent date, and decided date. /quotes header carries an 'Export' menu; user picks 'This year (CSV)'; the file downloads with a deterministic filename.

persona.name: Diego Martínez
persona.context: Bookkeeper asked for everything quoted in 2026.

preconditions: Account has 100+ quotes spanning the year, mixed statuses

steps.action: Loads /quotes, opens the page-header 'Export' menu, picks 'This year (CSV)'
steps.expect: Browser starts a download named 'quotes-2026.csv' (no millisecond suffix; deterministic for the year)
First row is a header: id,customer,summary,status,total,sentAt,decidedAt,decidedReason; money column is dollars-as-decimal (e.g. 350.00) for spreadsheet ergonomics, NOT cents
Dates parse as dates; money parses as currency; UTF-8 names with accents survive; commas inside descriptions are properly quoted

evaluationCriteria.check: Export menu item uses the same chevron/icon vocabulary as the rest of the page header
Bookkeeper-friendly — file opens cleanly in Excel/Numbers without import-wizard fights
Money is fmtMoneyExact-style (dollars.cents) at the boundary; cents-shape internal stays internal
Menu is keyboard-navigable; download starts on Enter; no focus loss after the download starts
